description: Servlet with multiple vuln types: command injection, SQL injection via concat, and file resource leak

expected.notes: AST pattern detects string concatenation inside executeQuery argument
request.getParameter("input") concatenated into SQL query passed to executeQuery
FileInputStream opened on line 23 but never closed
Runtime.getRuntime().exec(input) in exec branch; AST pattern correctly matches
source at 11:9 (request.getParameter("input")) flows through SQL query (line 17) into result set output at out.println(rs.getString(1)); second-order taint via tainted query results

response.getWriter().println(new String(data)) — file-read bytes, not reflected request input, must NOT raise reflected-XSS (Cap::HTML_ESCAPE). Regression guard retargeted from the retired java.xss.getwriter_print AST pattern to the taint sink that now owns reflected XSS.
